title: Diploma in Electric Engineering (Electric Vehicle)
content: The program is designed to provide a comprehensive exposure to the skill set required for the EV revolution. This skill set includes electric vehicle technology, power electronic for electric vehicles, Testing and certification of electric and hybrid vehicles. It incorporates fundamental and practical aspects of the relevant devices and technologies. 10th with minimum 40% marks. For Lateral Entry: Or 10th (minimum 40%) + 2 year ITI (minimum 40%) shall be eligible for admission to Second Year Diploma Course(s) in Any branch of Engineering and Technology. The programme is designed to provide a comprehensive exposure to the skill set required for the EV revolution. This skill set includes electric vehicle technology, power electronic for electric vehicles, Testing and certification of electric and hybrid vehicles. It incorporates fundamental and practical aspects of the relevant devices and technologies. Program Structure01 A Diploma in Electrical Engineering (Electric Vehicle is a three-year diploma course that offers students the knowledge and skills required to design, and optimize, electrical and digital systems of motor vehicles, with a special focus on electric and hybrid vehicles. 01 Basic knowledge: An ability to apply knowledge of basic mathematics, science, and engineering to solve engineering problems. 02 Discipline knowledge: An ability to apply discipline-specific knowledge to solve core and/or applied engineering problems. 03 04 Engineering tools: Apply appropriate technologies and tools with an understanding of the limitations. 05 06 Environment and sustainability: Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development. 07 08 Individual and team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings. 09 Communication: An ability to communicate effectively. 10 01 Ability to design, develop, and integrate EV systems, including powertrains, battery management, and power electronics, using simulation tools while ensuring safety and compliance. 02 Skills to perform advanced diagnostics, maintenance, and troubleshooting of EV systems, using modern diagnostic tools and safe handling of high-voltage components. 03 Capability to integrate sustainable energy solutions in EVs and gain industry-relevant experience through hands-on training, projects, and internships. 10th with minimum 40% marks. For Lateral Entry: Or 10th (minimum 40%) + 2 year ITI (minimum 40%) shall be eligible for admission to Second Year Diploma Course(s) in Any branch of Engineering and Technology.
